workflow.title: Workflows
workflow.subtitle: State machine engine and business processes
workflow.no_instances_desc: Instances are created when a business object triggers a published workflow.
workflow.publish_confirm: Publish this workflow? It will become available to create instances.
workflow.editor_hint: Drag and drop states and transitions to build your processes
workflow.no_workflow_filtered_desc: Adjust filters or search to find a workflow.
workflow.no_workflow_desc: Create your first workflow to automate business processes.
workflow.safe_system_mode: Protected system workflow: read-only structure, editable transition settings.
workflow.entity_type_help: Associates this workflow with a specific business object type.
workflow.definition_load_error: Unable to load this workflow definition.
workflow.catalog_description: System workflows drive critical modules. Their structure stays locked to avoid breaking orchestration; only safe settings can be changed.
workflow.catalog_hint: If you need to change the deep business logic, start from a clone. If you are adjusting an approver role, a permission, a condition, or an SLA, you can stay on the system workflow.
workflow.system_section_desc: Official FSM baseline used by business modules
workflow.custom_published_desc: Active versions specific to the entity
workflow.archives_desc: Definitions removed from the active cycle
workflow.delete_confirm_message: The workflow "{{name}}" will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone.
workflow.label: Label
workflow.required_roles_help: The user must hold at least one of these roles to validate this step.
workflow.expression_help: Boolean expression evaluated with instance data.
workflow.template_help: Identifier of the email/notification template to send.
workflow.system_check_help: Name of the system hook called automatically at this step.
workflow.node_locked_help: This system workflow structure is locked. Steps cannot be deleted or renamed here.
workflow.condition_help: Expression evaluated to allow the automatic transition.
workflow.required_role_help: Only users with this role can trigger this transition.
workflow.transition_locked_help: Protected system transition. You can adjust roles, permissions, assignment, conditions and SLA, but not delete or reroute this transition.

validation.start_required: The workflow must contain a start node
validation.end_required: The workflow must contain at least one end node
validation.min_nodes: The workflow must contain at least 2 nodes
validation.min_edges: The workflow must contain at least one transition
validation.missing_source: Transition references a missing source node: '{{source}}'
validation.missing_target: Transition references a missing target node: '{{target}}'
validation.unconnected_node: Node "{{node}}" is not connected to any transition
validation.invalid_condition: Transition "{{edge}}": condition cannot be executed by the workflow engine
validation.start_incoming: The start node should not have incoming transitions
validation.end_outgoing: End node "{{node}}" should not have outgoing transitions

imputations.assignment_priority_description: Assignments are resolved by context and priority before the entity fallback applies.
imputations.settings_hint_description: The imputation registry is visible and operable as a first-class business module.

validation.invalid_priority: Priority score must be a non-negative integer.

requester.title: Request a stay or prepare a mission
requester.description: Start with a visit notice if you manage a stay. Use an AVM if you need to structure a mission with several steps or linked visit notices.

guidance.add_pax: Add PAX, then review destination, dates and imputation before submission.
guidance.use_avm: Use an AVM when you need to prepare a mission with several activities, sites or linked visit notices.
guidance.imputation_rule: Validators can still adjust the imputation before approval, but the internal requester entry remains the initial source of truth.

validator.title: Process dossiers, arbitrate gaps, keep departures moving
validator.description: This view prioritizes visit notices and mission notices that require validation, compliance review or arbitration before movement.

empty.ads_priority: No immediate visit notice in queue.

guidance.ads_priority: Handle `pending_validation` visit notices first, then `pending_compliance` dossiers when business arbitration is required.
guidance.ads_imputation: On a visit notice, imputation can still be adjusted before final validation; what matters is dossier, PAX and destination consistency.
guidance.avm_scope: On an AVM, the objective is to validate mission framing and the lines that will later trigger visit notices or revisions.

ads.validator_hint_prefix: Validation queue: start with dossiers in

empty.validator: No visit notice in this queue

avm.validator_hint_prefix: Mission arbitration queue: prioritize AVMs in

create_ads.title: New visit notice
create_ads.subtitle: PaxLog
create_ads.intro: Create the visit notice first with destination, period and purpose. You will add PAX, imputation and attachments afterwards in the dossier.

placeholders.visit_purpose: Describe the purpose of the visit...

create_ads.footer_hint: Passengers and imputations can be added after creation from the detail panel.
create_ads.allowed_companies_help: Use this scope to limit external autocomplete, public links, and audit visibility to the selected companies.

help.request_description: Declare who initiated the ADS. OpsFlux prefills the current user, but a planner may file on behalf of someone else.
help.request_tip_requester: The requester will receive notifications and validation routing.
help.type_destination_description: Pick the visit shape (individual or team) and the entry site involved.
help.type_individual: Single PAX. Simplified flow, no team handling.
help.type_team: Multiple PAX sharing the same window and site. Site access is handled as a team.
help.visit_details_description: Spell out the purpose, dates and visit category — required for compliance validation.
help.visit_tip_purpose: Be precise: who, what, why. This is what ships to the site on the PDF.
help.visit_tip_dates: Dates drive POB conflicts and transport availability.
help.visit_tip_category: Category (project work, maintenance, inspection, visit, permanent ops, other) drives the validation workflow.
help.allowed_companies_description: Optional — restrict which companies can use the public boarding link and external autocomplete.
help.passengers_description: Add PAX at creation time to avoid round-trips. Internal PAX (OpsFlux users) and external (third-party contacts) are both supported.
help.passengers_tip_search: Search federates internal users and third-party contacts — type a name or a company.
help.passengers_tip_external: For someone not yet referenced, create the contact in Tiers first and come back here.
help.attachments_description: Supporting documents (visa, invitation letter, host company approval).
help.notes_description: Internal notes visible only in OpsFlux.
help.imputations_description: Cost split by BU / cost center / project.

create_avm.title: New mission notice
create_avm.subtitle: PaxLog — Mission notices (AVM)
create_avm.intro: The AVM is used to frame a mission before generating or linking visit notices. Describe the mission, its timeframe and its program lines.

checklist.title: Mission title completed
checklist.program_line: At least one program line described

create_avm.date_hint: The mission timeframe is the baseline. Program lines can then refine their own dates.
create_avm.program_intro: Start with the main mission steps. You can later arbitrate, link visit notices and refine lines during validation.

external_link.channel_hint: The effective channel is determined by the admin OTP configuration and the contact details available for the selected PAX.
external_link.no_allowed_companies: Add an allowed company to enable the external portal.
external_link.no_recipient: No PAX on this AdS has a usable email address or phone number for OTP delivery.
external_link.audit_description: Security view of external links already issued for this AdS.

compliance.compliant: Compliance is OK for the selected site.

reject.passenger_placeholder: Optional reason for rejecting this passenger

request_review.placeholder: Explain what must be corrected before resubmission...

stay_change.title: Stay change request
stay_change.reason_placeholder: Explain why the dates or purpose must be revised while the stay is already engaged...

ads_detail.round_trip_no_overnight_hint: Single-day visit — counted in the PAX forecast for the visit day only, with no overnight POB consumption.

next_action.draft_ready: The dossier can be submitted. Review PAX and imputation before sending.
next_action.draft_missing: Complete the missing information and add at least one passenger before submission.
next_action.submitted: The dossier is waiting for compliance review or validation.
next_action.pending_initiator_review: The dossier is waiting for confirmation from the actual requester before the validation flow can continue.
next_action.pending_compliance: The dossier is under compliance review. Monitor passenger and document gaps.
next_action.pending_project_review: The dossier is still waiting for {{count}} project approval(s) before compliance and site validation.
next_action.pending_validation: The dossier is under validation. Final adjustments mostly concern imputation and arbitration.
next_action.requires_review: The dossier was sent back for correction. Apply the requested changes and resubmit it.
next_action.approved: The stay is approved. Prepare movements and, if needed, share the external link.
next_action.in_progress: The stay is in progress. Any date or return change must be re-arbitrated.
next_action.completed: The stay is completed. The dossier now serves as reference and audit trail.
next_action.rejected: The dossier was rejected. Correct the content or create a new request depending on the reason.
next_action.cancelled: The dossier is cancelled. No operational action is expected.

readiness.imputation_hint: Imputation is not blocking at creation time. It still must be reviewed before final dossier approval.

ads_detail.search_pax_placeholder: Search for a user, contact, PAX profile...

stay_programs.empty: No stay program defined for this visit notice.

operational_impact.stay_change: This visit notice was sent back to review after an in-flight stay change.
operational_impact.avm_modified: This visit notice is under review following a change to the source AVM.
operational_impact.avm_cancelled: This visit notice was impacted by the cancellation of the source AVM.
operational_impact.planner_modified: This visit notice is under review following a change to the linked Planner activity.
operational_impact.planner_cancelled: This visit notice was impacted by the cancellation of the linked Planner activity.

next_action.draft_ready: The mission can be submitted to start preparation and validations.
next_action.draft_missing: Complete mission framing and program lines before submission.
next_action.in_preparation: Follow preparation tasks, then validate linked visit notices before final arbitration.
next_action.active: The mission is in progress. Monitor lines, generated visit notices and date changes.
next_action.ready: The mission is ready for final arbitration. Approval can now be launched.
next_action.completed: Mission completed. Use this view as the reference dossier.
next_action.cancelled: Mission cancelled. Keep the dossier for audit and history.

profile_panel.subtitle: PaxLog

type_help.internal: Internal staff — linked to a user account
type_help.external: Contractor / visitor — linked to a company (tier)

profile_panel.promoted_external_user_inactive: Contact promoted to inactive external user
profile_panel.external_contact_promoted: External contact profile promoted to external user
profile_panel.reference_evidence_help: Add the profile and reference supporting files here, for example a medical certificate, attestations, qualifications, or any proof required for compliance verification.

placeholders.owner_id: UUID or business identifier of the owner

errors.missing_required: Fill in the type, owner and owner identifier.

records.create_attachment_hint: After creation, the record stays open so the required supporting files can be uploaded immediately.
records.attachments_help: Upload the proof for the declared reference here: certificate, attestation, signed copy, scan, or any other supporting evidence.

help.severity_description: Pick the severity — it drives downstream actions (notifications, access blocks, duration).
help.severity_info: Informational entry kept in the PAX history. No automatic action.
help.severity_warning: Warning visible to planners. The PAX is still authorized.
help.severity_site_ban: Access denied on a specific site. You must select the asset.
help.severity_temp_ban: Access denied for a bounded period. Start and end dates are required.
help.severity_permanent_ban: Permanent denial. The PAX cannot be boarded while the incident is active.
help.concerned_description: Identify who is concerned. An incident can target an individual, a whole company, or a PAX group.
help.concerned_tip_pax: Most common case — an individual behavior.
help.concerned_tip_company: To sanction a company (chronic delays, rule breaches).
help.concerned_tip_group: To sanction a PAX group (team, shift, site).
help.details_description: Factual detail of the incident. The description is a rich field (images, lists, tables).
help.details_tip_date: Today's date is prefilled — change it if the incident happened earlier.
help.details_tip_description: Stay factual: who, when, where, how. Emotions will not help later investigation.
help.ban_period_description: Time bounds of the ban. A temporary ban needs both dates; a permanent ban only needs the start date.
help.attachments_description: Pictures, reports, emails tied to the incident.
help.notes_description: Internal notes visible only in OpsFlux.

help.general_description: Identify the activity and its operational context (title, site, linked project).
help.general_tip_title: Prefer a clear business title — avoid pure technical codes.
help.general_tip_site: Site is mandatory — it drives POB availability and validation rules.
help.general_tip_project: Linking the activity to a project helps Gantt progress rollup.
help.type_description: Pick the activity type (project, workover, drilling, maintenance, etc.) — it unlocks specific fields (well, depth, work order).
help.type_tip_pob: Variable POB mode lets you model day-by-day POB plans; useful for long campaigns.
help.planning_description: Activity time window. Dates drive POB conflicts and scheduling.
help.description_description: Rich description — images, tables, links. Type "/" to open the Notion-like block menu.
help.notes_description: Internal notes visible only in OpsFlux.
help.tags_description: Tags to filter in the list and reports.
help.workover_description: Workover-specific details — well reference and rig name.
help.drilling_description: Drilling-specific details — spud date, target depth, program reference.
help.maintenance_description: Regulatory details — regulatory reference, work order.

planner.voir_toutes_les_vues_en_mode_scenario: View all views in scenario mode
planner.pour_les_voir_dans_le_gantt_ou: pour les voir dans le Gantt, ou
planner.aucune_modification_ou_nouvelle_activite: No change or new activity in this scenario
planner.un_scenario_est_un_plan_alternatif_et_si: — A scenario is an alternative plan ("what if?"). It does not touch the current plan.
planner.cliquez_ajouter_pour_creer_des_activites: — Click "Add" to create activities specific to this scenario, or from the Gantt, select an activity and choose "Add to scenario".
planner.lance_un_calcul_de_conflits_de_capacite: — Runs a capacity-conflict calculation to assess the scenario's feasibility.
planner.passer_a_valide_pour_review_puis_promouv: — Switch to "Validated" for review, then "Promote" converts the scenario activities into real activities in the plan.
planner.si_un_scenario_promu_pose_probleme_resta: — If a promoted scenario causes problems, "Restore" reverts the promotion and returns to the previous state.
planner.copier_la_premiere_cellule_sur_toute_la: Copy the first cell across the selection

travelwiz.title: TravelWiz
travelwiz.subtitle: Travel, vector and logistics rotation management
travelwiz.voyages_empty_description: Plan and track personnel travel to operational sites.
travelwiz.vectors_empty_description: Manage transport assets: helicopters, boats, vehicles and other logistics vectors.
travelwiz.cargo_empty_description: Track material and equipment shipments to sites.
travelwiz.manifests_empty_description: Manage passenger and cargo manifests for each rotation.
travelwiz.rotations_empty_description: Plan personnel rotations and on-site work cycles.

rotation.help_identification: Identify the rotation with a business-meaningful name, the assigned vector and the departure base. Those three fields define the nominal logistics contract.
rotation.help_identification_tip_name: Use a descriptive name reflecting the route and frequency (e.g. "Pointe-Noire Weekly").
rotation.help_identification_tip_vector: The vector (helicopter, ship, commercial flight…) must be active and available for the window.
rotation.help_identification_tip_base: The departure base drives every voyage generated from this rotation.
rotation.help_periodicity: The rotation defines the nominal cadence. Operational voyages remain concrete occurrences generated or planned from it.
rotation.help_periodicity_tip_cron: Use the CRON assistant to describe the recurrence (weekday, hour, interval).
rotation.help_periodicity_tip_voyage: Each occurrence will generate a voyage you can edit independently.
rotation.help_attachments: Contractual documents tied to the rotation (framework agreement, logistics convention, etc.).
rotation.help_notes: Internal notes visible only in OpsFlux.

travelwiz.aucun_cargo_affecte_a_ce_voyage: No cargo assigned to this voyage.
travelwiz.renseigne_latitude_et_longitude_pour_vis: Renseigne latitude et longitude pour visualiser le point de pickup sur la carte.

delete_policies.title: Delete Policies
delete_policies.description: Configure deletion behavior for each entity type.

help.title: Settings
help.description: Centralizes profile setup, personal preferences, security, and the application settings that can be administered.
help.profile_description: The Profile section is where users maintain their identity, contact details, and personal reference data. Complete it first so workflows, signatures, notifications, and compliance checks can rely on accurate information.

profile_workflow.title: Configure your profile
steps: Open Settings, then the Profile tab.
Fill in your identity, contact details, position, and any user record information required by your organization.
Add or update addresses, secondary emails, and other contact methods if your organization uses them.
Verify that your photo, language, and personal preferences match your day-to-day usage.
Save your changes, then review the completeness score or status if it is displayed.

notifications_workflow.title: Tune your notifications
steps: Open the Notifications tab to choose which channels are used per event or per module.
Keep only the alerts that are operationally useful to reduce noise.
Then review the Preferences tab for language, appearance, and personal behaviors.
Validate the setup on a real event or with a known notification.

security_workflow.title: Secure your access
steps: Use the Password tab to rotate your access secrets.
Review Active Sessions and close the ones that should no longer remain open.
Create access tokens only when there is an explicit and limited need.
Revoke any token or connected application as soon as it is no longer needed.

tips: An up-to-date profile improves signatures, notifications, and compliance checks.
Personal preferences affect your user experience, but they do not replace permissions managed by administrators.
Review your active sessions and tokens regularly if you use OpsFlux across multiple devices.

fields.objectives_ph: Brief description of the change objectives
fields.transition_comment_ph: Optional comment attached to the transition
fields.installation_hint: Link to asset registry site/installation if known — optional
fields.installation_hint_v2: Pick the installation from the registry — site and platform are auto-derived
fields.manual_location_toggle: Manual entry (installation not yet in the registry)
fields.duration_days_hint: Optional — start/end dates take precedence for reminders.
fields.moc_type_hint: {{count}} validation rule(s) will be auto-seeded from this type.

fields.metiers_empty: Dictionary moc_metier is empty — seed or edit it.

create.title: New MOC
create.subtitle: Change request
create.validators_hint: These validators are added on top of the automatic matrix seeded from the MOC type (if defined).

help.location_description: Locate the change. The recommended path is the installation picker (Asset registry) — site and platform are derived automatically from the hierarchy.
help.location_tip_installation: If the installation exists in the registry, prefer selecting it over typing manually.
help.location_tip_fallback: Use manual entry only when the installation is not yet referenced.
help.initiator_description: Identify the person who initiated the MOC. An internal OpsFlux email is enough; an external mode is available for contractors.
help.initiator_tip_email: The email will feed subsequent notifications and validations.
help.initiator_tip_external: Tick "external" for a contractor outside OpsFlux.
help.initiator_tip_signature: The signature is required to generate the regulatory PDF.
help.objectives_description: The heart of the request: describe the change and its context. All rich fields support images, tables and lists (try "/" to open the block menu).
help.objectives_tip_title: The short title is what appears in lists and email subjects.
help.objectives_tip_objectives: Keep the objective concise — a single line summarizing the why.
help.objectives_tip_rich: Current situation, proposed changes and impact analysis are rich text (Tiptap) fields.
help.type_description: Classify the MOC to drive the automatic validation matrix and regulatory planning.
help.type_tip_metiers: Tick the disciplines involved — each selected one adds a validator in the matrix.
help.nature_optimisation: Production or operability improvement without major regulatory change.
help.nature_securite: Change aimed at reducing an HSE risk or closing a safety gap.
help.modtype_permanent: Permanent change — the MOC stays applicable until a further modification.
help.modtype_temporary: Time-bounded change with mandatory end date and lift workflow.
help.validators_description: Add ad-hoc validators on top of the automatic matrix derived from the MOC type. Useful to invite a specialist or a representative from another department.
help.role_production_manager: Operations manager of the impacted site.
help.role_maintenance_manager: Maintenance manager — impact on maintenance plan.
help.role_process_engineer: Junior process engineer — drafting the conclusions.

delete.confirm_message: MOC {{ref}} will be archived. An administrator can undo.

section.execution_accord_hint: Both directors must give their accord before execution starts on site.

reminders.title: Temporary MOC reminders
reminders.description: Temporary MOCs nearing their end date trigger an automatic email + in-app reminder. Configure the thresholds (J-N) before expiry.
reminders.enabled_hint: Turn the daily job on / off.
reminders.thresholds_hint: Add as many milestones as needed. Default: J-30, J-14, J-7, J-1.

types.title: MOC types
types.description: Catalogue of MOC categories with their default validation matrix. When a MOC is created with a type, the matrix is auto-seeded.
types.empty: No type defined yet. Create one to make it available in the creation form.
types.rules_empty: No rules. Add the roles that must validate this MOC type.

help.none: No help available for this page.
